story.title: Checkout — Reject expired credit card
story.description: When a customer reaches the payment step of checkout and enters a card whose MM/YY expiry is in the past, the application must reject the payment without contacting the processor, show a specific error, and leave the cart intact.

acceptance_criteria: Given a card whose expiry is in any past month (any year), when the customer submits the payment form, then the application rejects the payment client-side or at the order service boundary, before any processor call is made.
The customer sees the error message 'This card has expired. Please use a different card.' No other error variant is acceptable.
The cart contents (items, quantities, discount code if any) are preserved and visible when the user returns to the cart.
The order log records the rejection with the reason 'expired_card_client_reject' and 'processor_called=false'.

ambiguities.description: The story says 'client-side OR at the order service boundary' — both are acceptable. The Test Designer must cover both layers, not pick one and ignore the other.

risks.description: A card is charged anyway because the expiry check runs after the processor call (the regression this story exists to prevent).
The error message becomes generic or non-actionable, leading to abandoned carts.
The cart is cleared on rejection, forcing the user to re-add items.
